rationale: The citing paper explicitly says it extends the visual-inference literature and uses visual-attention outcomes (including an eye-tracking study on appearance-related AOIs), while the cited JMR paper is directly about how static visual design cues (logo dynamism) shape consumer responses and also uses eye-tracking. Given this clear conceptual/method overlap, the citation is plausibly a substantive theory/mechanism or method source rather than an obvious incidental mention.
